title: Right-censored time-to-next-stop model converted to next-lap hazard
summary: XGBoost’s AFT survival objective is designed for time-to-event labels with censoring.
rationale: XGBoost’s AFT survival objective is designed for time-to-event labels with censoring. This maps naturally to rows near race end where the next stop is unobserved because the stint finishes without another pit. That is a cleaner formulation than plain binary classification when the same driver contributes many pre-stop rows with different horizons.
implementation_hint: For each row, build `time_to_next_pit` within `(Race, Year, Driver)`; if no later pit exists, mark it right-censored at laps remaining. Train an AFT model on the same tabular features plus current baseline features, then convert predicted survival to `P(stop on next lap)` or stack its raw score with the binary GBDT model using OOF predictions only.
expected_effect: Better handling of horizon information and end-of-race censoring; likely strongest on long stints and late-race rows where binary labels are noisy.
risk: If the AFT-to-next-lap conversion is crude, it may improve CV but not leaderboard. Needs strict race-wise sequence construction.
